posture: Quiet Thursday scan — no confirmable new, in-scope innovation signal surfaced in the last 24 hours. The sector's active frontiers (FOGO/circular economy, AI-assisted development assessment, smart communities) remain warm but produced no fresh, in-window council announcement today; the strongest recent 'adopt' material remains the 2026 National Awards for Local Government already logged on 29 June.
summary: Empty day. A full daily scan of the free §1a–§1d sources (AU council & sector press, AU industry verticals, overseas equal-weight outlets and the tertiary/research institutes) returned no item that was simultaneously (a) published in the last 24 hours, (b) in scope per the §3b Australian-council-function filter, and (c) not already covered in the previous 3 days. Recurring or undated items were again reviewed and excluded: the NSW FOGO rollout milestones (commercial source-separated organics from 1 July 2026; household mandate to 2030) are regulatory-rollout dates rather than fresh innovation signals; Hawkesbury City Council's ARC Ento Tech black-soldier-fly waste-to-resource trial and its associated FOGO deferral rest on prior-period (Nov 2025/Jan 2026) decisions; and the NSW AI-in-planning program (AI Solutions Panel, Early Adopter Grants, DAISY/Civica integration, ~70% of VIC councils exploring AI in statutory planning) produced no new dated development today. Awards-cycle material was reviewed but not logged as a fresh 'adopt' signal: the 2026 Local Government Excellence Awards finalist lists (e.g. Clarence Valley Council's Accelerated Development Application Service) are nominations rather than proven, transferable innovations, and the 2026 Active Australia Innovation Challenge winners (10 July) fall outside the 24-hour window. Overseas and network material (Bloomberg CityLab 2026 Madrid readouts and the 2025–26 Mayors Challenge winners, LGIU/LocalGov roads and reorganisation content, UK LCRIG items) was out-of-window, network/policy content, or already logged as background. Domestic energy items (1 July 2026 solar/battery incentive changes; private almond-orchard and Corryong microgrids) were state/private-sector rather than council-transferable signals. Today is a Thursday, so the §1e 50-council watchlist deep scan (Saturdays only) was not run. No paywalled items were logged; where a paid title was the only source it was held to headline + standfirst and not used. Sections are published empty per §6.
